title: thymus pulegioides subsp. pannonicus
intro: Thymus pulegioides subsp. pannonicus, also known as the Pannonian thyme, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which help to combat infections and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Pannonian thyme include reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. Pannonian thyme can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
